本单元入口
整套首页
unit_01 0% 正确率 0 复习 0 错题

unit_01

推荐练习默认不提示本单元词。需要复盘时可手动显示本单元词；提交后判分，也可放弃查看答案。

50 推荐题 327 全部题目 0 弱相关题 30% 推荐答案命中比例
#1 Sentence Equivalence
The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do ______ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.The beauty of the scientific approach is that even when individual researchers do ______ bias or partiality, others can correct them using a framework of evidence on which everyone broadly agrees.
答案：C, E　succumb to; yield to
biaspartiality
bias: standard unit headword | partiality: standard unit headword
#2 Text Completion
Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.Computers make it spectacularly easy to search for particular pieces of information in downloaded texts. And doing research in this strategic, targeted manner can feel (i)______. Instead of (ii)______ the organizing logic of the book you are reading, you can approach the book with your own questions and (iii)______. You, not the author, are the master.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, H　empowering; surrendering to; glean precisely what you want from it
masterempowerdisregard
master: standard unit headword | empower: standard unit headword | disregard: standard unit headword
#3 TC TRIPLE
The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.The purpose of the Endangered Species Act, which was signed into law in 1973, is to conserve species at risk of extinction and the ecosystems upon which these species depend. (i) __________ a requirement simply to prevent extinction, the Act entails an (ii) __________ mandate: to "use ... all methods and procedures which are necessary to bring any [listed] species ... to the point at which the measures provided pursuant to this [Act] are no longer necessary." According to the Act, in other words, conservation requires the (iii) __________ of listed species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　More than; affirmative; recovery
entailaccord
entail: standard unit headword | accord: standard unit headword
#4 TC DOUBLE
Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i)________ events, whereas weeds are (ii)________.Weeds lack the lethality of insects and microbes, which can strike farm crops seemingly out of nowhere and rapidly wipe them out. It is worth bearing in mind, however, that infestations of insects and disease are typically (i)________ events, whereas weeds are (ii)________.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　sporadic; ubiquitous
bearbearing
bear: standard unit headword | bearing: standard unit headword
#5 TC SINGLE
The composer's music had been criticized for being simple and accessible to a fault, but defenders countered that music need not be __________ to be great.The composer's music had been criticized for being simple and accessible to a fault, but defenders countered that music need not be __________ to be great.
答案：C　inscrutable
accessiblefault
accessible: standard unit headword | fault: standard unit headword
#6 Text Completion
Trying to fix problems that affect vast numbers of people has an intuitive appeal that politicians and policy makers find (i)______, but several warehouses of research studies show that intuition is often a poor guide to fixing (ii)______ problems.Trying to fix problems that affect vast numbers of people has an intuitive appeal that politicians and policy makers find (i)______, but several warehouses of research studies show that intuition is often a poor guide to fixing (ii)______ problems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　irresistible; systemic
intuitivesystemicirresistible
intuitive: standard unit headword | systemic: standard unit headword | irresistible: standard unit headword
#7 Sentence Equivalence
The Chavez Pass archaeological site was initially interpreted as indicative of ______ society, since it was thought to have been at the center of a cluster of smaller, contemporary settlements that it presumably controlled.The Chavez Pass archaeological site was initially interpreted as indicative of ______ society, since it was thought to have been at the center of a cluster of smaller, contemporary settlements that it presumably controlled.
答案：B, F　a hierarchical; a stratified
settlementcontemporary
settlement: standard unit headword | contemporary: standard unit headword
#8 TC SINGLE
A writing system that was not ____________ but deliberately formed on the basis of other scripts was introduced under the ancient Indian King Asoka, who ruled most of the Indian subcontinent from c. 268 to 232 B.C.E.A writing system that was not ____________ but deliberately formed on the basis of other scripts was introduced under the ancient Indian King Asoka, who ruled most of the Indian subcontinent from c. 268 to 232 B.C.E.
答案：D　indigenous
comprehensiveesoteric
comprehensive: standard unit headword | esoteric: standard unit headword
#9 SE
In a newly formed group of people, the hierarchy that emerges may be entirely __________, but group members will nevertheless tend to construct after-the-fact rationalizations for why those in charge should be in charge.In a newly formed group of people, the hierarchy that emerges may be entirely __________, but group members will nevertheless tend to construct after-the-fact rationalizations for why those in charge should be in charge.
答案：B, D　haphazard; arbitrary
chargearbitrary
charge: standard unit headword | arbitrary: standard unit headword
#10 TC DOUBLE
Although the system for collecting data about user needs is called a "customer capture method," this scientific-sounding terminology connotes greater (i) __________ than the method can deliver. While this terminology de-emphasizes the (ii) ___________ aspects of knowledge management processes, such elements abound in the actual data collection.Although the system for collecting data about user needs is called a "customer capture method," this scientific-sounding terminology connotes greater (i) __________ than the method can deliver. While this terminology de-emphasizes the (ii) ___________ aspects of knowledge management processes, such elements abound in the actual data collection.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　neutrality; interpretative and evaluative
de-emphasizeimpartial
de-emphasize: standard unit headword | impartial: standard unit headword
#11 Sentence Equivalence
Peoples’ decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal considerations and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often ______: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.Peoples’ decisions about childbearing depend on innumerable personal considerations and societal factors, yet even knowing this, demographers are often ______: their projections of birth rates frequently turn out to be embarrassingly at odds with reality.
答案：B, F　flummoxed; confounded
childbearingodds
childbearing: standard unit headword | odds: standard unit headword
#12 TC TRIPLE
The artists sought to (i) _________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) ________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) _________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.The artists sought to (i) _________ precisely the impulses that traditional or generally accepted thinking had (ii) ________. By cultivating instinctive, irrational awarenesses--which might come from dreams or from accepting results derived by chance or from a receptivity to taboo--they hoped to (iii) _________ their audience's sense of everyday reality.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　affirm; marginalized; unsettle
irrationalunsettle
irrational: standard unit headword | unsettle: standard unit headword
#13 SE
The Tigris and Euphrates rivers are known for being ____________: they flow with such a low gradient that they meander considerably and throw numerous side branches.The Tigris and Euphrates rivers are known for being ____________: they flow with such a low gradient that they meander considerably and throw numerous side branches.
答案：E, F　convoluted; intricate
gradient
gradient: standard unit headword
#14 TC SINGLE
Persian chronicles of India's seventeenth-century Mughal court often ___________ Sanskrit intellectuals' participation in court life, so scholars Who rely solely on Persian materials and mirror their sources generally disregard Sanskrit intellectuals' role.Persian chronicles of India's seventeenth-century Mughal court often ___________ Sanskrit intellectuals' participation in court life, so scholars Who rely solely on Persian materials and mirror their sources generally disregard Sanskrit intellectuals' role.
答案：B　elide
disregardmirror
disregard: standard unit headword | mirror: standard unit headword
#15 Text Completion
The economic recovery was somewhat lopsided: (i)______ in some of the industries while (ii)______ in others of them.The economic recovery was somewhat lopsided: (i)______ in some of the industries while (ii)______ in others of them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　feeble; robust
feeblelopsided
feeble: standard unit headword | lopsided: standard unit headword
#16 TC TRIPLE
The only way to definitively test a large civil engineering structure such as a bridge is to build it in anticipation of possible challenges from nature and then let nature take its course. That is not to say that every new large structure is a (i) __________. Engineers understand a great deal about the behavior and limitations of their structures' components. Furthermore, it is the (ii) __________ structure that is built more than 10 or 20 percent larger than its predecessors, thus allowing engineers to move (iii) __________ into unknown territory.The only way to definitively test a large civil engineering structure such as a bridge is to build it in anticipation of possible challenges from nature and then let nature take its course. That is not to say that every new large structure is a (i) __________. Engineers understand a great deal about the behavior and limitations of their structures' components. Furthermore, it is the (ii) __________ structure that is built more than 10 or 20 percent larger than its predecessors, thus allowing engineers to move (iii) __________ into unknown territory.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　gamble; unusual; incrementally
limitation
limitation: standard unit headword
#17 SE
After trimming parts of the anatomy of some flowers and gluing additions onto others, Wilson found that effective pollination produced despite a remarkable amount of variation in flower ______________.After trimming parts of the anatomy of some flowers and gluing additions onto others, Wilson found that effective pollination produced despite a remarkable amount of variation in flower ______________.
答案：C, E　form; architecture
remarkable
remarkable: standard unit headword

Section 4 of5|Question 2of 15 Mark Review Help Back Next Fillall blanks in the way that best completes the text, Although people sometimes conflate them, the Beat poetry scene and the folk-music revival were actually (i)________and in many ways(i)______,despite sharing certain ancestral connections in Depression-era left-wing politics.Section 4 of5|Question 2of 15 Mark Review Help Back Next Fillall blanks in the way that best completes the text, Although people sometimes conflate them, the Beat poetry scene and the folk-music revival were actually (i)________and in many ways(i)______,despite sharing certain ancestral connections in Depression-era left-wing politics.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　distinct; antagonistic
revival

The prosecutor belied his hard-boiled reputation by submitting (i) ______ queries to the witness and accepting in turn (ii) ______ responses.The prosecutor belied his hard-boiled reputation by submitting (i) ______ queries to the witness and accepting in turn (ii) ______ responses.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　innocuous; evasive
evasivequizzical
evasive: standard unit headword | quizzical: standard unit headword
#20 Sentence Equivalence
Today the chair of task force in charge of revising the psychiatric diagnostic manual is ______ post—people work for years to position themselves as candidates—but in the early 1970s, descriptive psychiatry was a backwater.Today the chair of task force in charge of revising the psychiatric diagnostic manual is ______ post—people work for years to position themselves as candidates—but in the early 1970s, descriptive psychiatry was a backwater.
答案：A, E　a coveted; an enviable
backwatercharge
backwater: standard unit headword | charge: standard unit headword
#21 TC SINGLE
Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would ____________ but seldom communicate.Needing to conceal their increasing alienation from each other, the family settled into what amounted to voluble nonsense, in that they would ____________ but seldom communicate.
答案：C　natter
settled
settled: standard unit headword
#22 Sentence Equivalence
The uniquely human ability to rethink and revise our social arrangements is a weird blessing, allowing us to create systems that are as likely to ______ us as to liberate us.The uniquely human ability to rethink and revise our social arrangements is a weird blessing, allowing us to create systems that are as likely to ______ us as to liberate us.
答案：B, E　shackle; stifle
blessingshackle
blessing: standard unit headword | shackle: standard unit headword
#23 TC TRIPLE
Although often seen as simply an inefficiency, redundancy in language actually serves as the (i) __________ to (ii) __________. It provides second chances, in other words. Every natural language has redundancy built in; this is why people can understand text (iii) __________ and why they can understand conversation in a noisy room.Although often seen as simply an inefficiency, redundancy in language actually serves as the (i) __________ to (ii) __________. It provides second chances, in other words. Every natural language has redundancy built in; this is why people can understand text (iii) __________ and why they can understand conversation in a noisy room.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, G　antidote; confusion; riddled with errors
precursor
precursor: standard unit headword
#24 TC DOUBLE
Although critics and curators have placed Romare Bearden in any number of contexts—with collage artists, with Black Atlantic artists, among American Abstractionists, or with artists from the Civil Rights era—he (i)_______ the (ii)_______ of the conventional art world.Although critics and curators have placed Romare Bearden in any number of contexts—with collage artists, with Black Atlantic artists, among American Abstractionists, or with artists from the Civil Rights era—he (i)_______ the (ii)_______ of the conventional art world.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　confounds; taxonomies
explicate
explicate: standard unit headword
#25 SE
Nigerian painter Uzo Egonu’s paintings evolved in the 1960s, becoming more planar and decorative by _______ depth and perspective.Nigerian painter Uzo Egonu’s paintings evolved in the 1960s, becoming more planar and decorative by _______ depth and perspective.
答案：B, D　relegating; subordinating
condone
condone: standard unit headword

Williams finds the appearance of Whitman’s Leaves of Grass in 1855 nearly ______ given the immense disparity between Whitman’s earlier published works, which Williams finds dismal, and the consummate mastery of Leaves.Williams finds the appearance of Whitman’s Leaves of Grass in 1855 nearly ______ given the immense disparity between Whitman’s earlier published works, which Williams finds dismal, and the consummate mastery of Leaves.
答案：D, F　inexplicable; incomprehensible
inexplicabledismal
inexplicable: standard unit headword | dismal: standard unit headword

As a longtime fan of the fashion photography website, I wasn’t sure if I wanted to see its images presented in a book, with all of the (i)______ that implies. What I’ve always enjoyed about the photos is their (ii)______: they are disposable snapshots of what’s going on in the world of style right now.As a longtime fan of the fashion photography website, I wasn’t sure if I wanted to see its images presented in a book, with all of the (i)______ that implies. What I’ve always enjoyed about the photos is their (ii)______: they are disposable snapshots of what’s going on in the world of style right now.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, E　permanency; ephemeral
snapshot
snapshot: standard unit headword

In line with a vow he had made to himself as a young man to remain __________ to all but his most intimate friends, Sacher cultivated an air of mystery and intrigue in public.In line with a vow he had made to himself as a young man to remain __________ to all but his most intimate friends, Sacher cultivated an air of mystery and intrigue in public.
答案：C　inscrutable
intrigue

Although Gwendolyn Brooks's work is, on the whole, __________, in some earlier works, intense linguistic and semantic compression occasionally present minor difficulties.Although Gwendolyn Brooks's work is, on the whole, __________, in some earlier works, intense linguistic and semantic compression occasionally present minor difficulties.
答案：A, B　rarely inaccessible; fairly comprehensible
comprehensible

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　sketchy; an exhaustive
misguided

Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.Seigel has written an important and invaluable book on the history of Western ideas of the self, showing a genuine (i) __________ in analyzing philosophical views of often daunting intricacy. Though a historian by profession, he keeps to a minimum his excursions into the (ii) __________ of ideas, recognizing that great minds often (iii) __________ the preconceptions of their age.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　acuity; social and cultural context; outstrip
timidity
timidity: standard unit headword
#32 Text Completion
Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.Human-caused disturbances, such as habitat destruction and the introduction of nonnative species, are among the leading causes of plant and animal population declines. Most populations are affected by a combination of adverse human pressures, each of which is in itself insufficient to (i)______ a population crash. Therefore, studies of population decline that (ii)______ individual factors and thus (iii)______ potential interactions may lead to improper management of declining species.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, H　trigger; focus on; overlook
trigger
trigger: standard unit headword

Enlarged sensory organs in cave-dwelling organisms are not necessarily the rule: the phreatic Texas blind catfish has actually developed __________ barbels, whisker-like sensory organs near the mouth that are a prominent feature of other catfish species.Enlarged sensory organs in cave-dwelling organisms are not necessarily the rule: the phreatic Texas blind catfish has actually developed __________ barbels, whisker-like sensory organs near the mouth that are a prominent feature of other catfish species.
答案：B　minute
intimidate
intimidate: standard unit headword

The thin glass at the end of their tails is (ii) __________, however-if you break it, the fracture will propagate through the drop at 2,000 miles per hour.Capable of withstanding repeated hammer blows, the heads of the tadpole-shaped glass droplets known as Prince Rupert's drops are highly (i) __________. The thin glass at the end of their tails is (ii) __________, however-if you break it, the fracture will propagate through the drop at 2,000 miles per hour.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　resilient; more vulnerable
homogeneous
homogeneous: standard unit headword

Because Earth's atmosphere distorts astronomical observations, whatever tends to __________ its influence is dismaying to the astronomer.Because Earth's atmosphere distorts astronomical observations, whatever tends to __________ its influence is dismaying to the astronomer.
答案：A, F　amplify; strengthen
revive
revive: standard unit headword

She was by nature (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　indifferent to; neither bookish nor cerebral
cerebral

答案：D, F　realism; verisimilitude
instrumental
instrumental: standard unit headword

答案：C　portentous
overblown

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　the gold standard; expense
unattainable

答案：B　documentary
ornamental

答案：C, D　irresolution; vacillation
vacillation

答案：D　linked
disorganized

transmogrify

答案：B　omnipresent
omnipresent
omnipresent: standard unit headword

The only (iii)______ sensory deprivation of the sessions came from the handsome color slides favored by the corporate presenters.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3

deprivation

答案：C, F　uncertainty; instability
coherence

答案：D, F　capture; secure

Blank 1
Blank 2

wistful

#50 Text Completion

答案：A　experimental
formulaiccontemporary

答案：C, F　a nebulous; a vague
palpable
palpable: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　hailed; acclaimed
hail

One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are ______ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.One view of historicism holds that systems of belief prevalent during different period in history are ______ and therefore cannot be understood except in their own term.
答案：B　incommensurable
discriminatory

答案：A, C　antediluvian; archaic
chauvinistic
chauvinistic: standard unit headword
#55 Text Completion

答案：A　deliberate
indiscriminate
indiscriminate: standard unit headword
#56 Text Completion
Although not enough to ______ the conventional view of the manuscript’s provenance, the new study was thought to have weakened the prevailing theory considerably.Although not enough to ______ the conventional view of the manuscript’s provenance, the new study was thought to have weakened the prevailing theory considerably.
答案：E　topple
topple
topple: standard unit headword
#57 Sentence Equivalence
For parents, the pleasure of letting children choose which book to read aloud together is not always ______: I well remembered my inner groans when my child would constantly pick my least favorite book from the shelf.For parents, the pleasure of letting children choose which book to read aloud together is not always ______: I well remembered my inner groans when my child would constantly pick my least favorite book from the shelf.
答案：D, F　unalloyed; unqualified

答案：C, F　discern; detect
envisage

#59 Text Completion
Saul’s particular combination of intellectuality and vitality was not paradoxical; it was category shattering. (i)______ was, in a way, his very theme. Was ever a bookish soul so cracklingly unmediated, so (ii)______ raw life? He was as vivid physically as he was mentally, almost perversely alert, completely at home in the world of matter, repulsed by (iii)______.Saul’s particular combination of intellectuality and vitality was not paradoxical; it was category shattering. (i)______ was, in a way, his very theme. Was ever a bookish soul so cracklingly unmediated, so (ii)______ raw life? Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　Energy; flush with; tedium

#60 Text Completion
Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)______, the most (ii)______ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii) ______. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of a proton.Gravitational waves—ripples in the geometry of space-time—are analogous to electromagnetic waves. The challenge in trying to observe these waves directly is that they are extremely weak. To make waves large enough to be (i)______, the most (ii)______ events in the universe are required: supernova explosions, the formation of black holes, or the collision of stars. Even so, the effects are (iii) ______. The geometry changes so little that a distance of several kilometers changes by less than the diameter of a proton.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, I　detectable; violent; minuscule
explicable
explicable: standard unit headword

答案：A, C　senescence; decrepitude

Although thawing permafrost often contributes to carbon emissions by releasing methane, active methanotrophs (a specialized group of microorganisms) utilize methane, ___________ declines in net emissions under extended periods of warm temperature.Although thawing permafrost often contributes to carbon emissions by releasing methane, active methanotrophs (a specialized group of microorganisms) utilize methane, ___________ declines in net emissions under extended periods of warm temperature.
答案：B　effecting

mirror: standard unit headword
#108 TC TRIPLE
Among chess aficionados, there have been many doomsday scenarios about people (i)________ the game as a result of the superiority of computer chess programs over human players. Others have replied to this with variation on the theme of how we still hold footraces despite cars going much faster, but this analogy is (ii)________ since cars do not help humans run faster while chess computers undoubtedly have an effect on the (iii)________ of human chess.Among chess aficionados, there have been many doomsday scenarios about people (i)________ the game as a result of the superiority of computer chess programs over human players. Others have replied to this with variation on the theme of how we still hold footraces despite cars going much faster, but this analogy is (ii)________ since cars do not help humans run faster while chess computers undoubtedly have an effect on the (iii)________ of human chess.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　losing interest in; spurious; quality

master: standard unit headword

答案：A, F　unwise; misguided

答案：E　haphazard

Blank 1
Blank 2

Granted, it would be (ii) to claim that the poem is self-contained or that its proliferating allusions are self-explanatory. For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.For many years, Americans have had a love affair with ferryboats. Ferries are said to relieve our frayed nerves after we’ve stewed in bumper-to-bumper traffic, and conventional wisdom also says ferries (i)______ congestion and air pollution by getting us out of our cars. Unfortunately, this (ii)______ notion recently has (iii)______ several West Coast mayors, who have in consequence eagerly pursued the implementation of ferry service in their cities.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　reduce; misguided; captivated

答案：B　subjective

The (i) establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) . While the media routinely report on scans showing that specific brain locations light up when we feel particular emotions or perform particular actions, the impression given by these news stories—that current technology provides real insights into how the brain works-is(iii) .Researchers have tried, with little success, to gain an understanding of how brain activity in roundworms gives rise to various rudimentary behaviors. The (i) establishing a link between biology and behavior in humans is (ii) . 答案：A, F　exhaustive; comprehensive

答案：D　topographically complex

Unlike news reports, theater isn’t expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i)_______, the form, by nature traffics in (ii)_______. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what’s real and what’s (iii)_______.Unlike news reports, theater isn’t expected to stick to the facts. Built on a sandy foundation of make-believe and (i)_______, the form, by nature traffics in (ii)_______. Good documentary drama exploits its inherent paradox: creating artifice from verbatim texts, it uncovers truths by playing on the tension between what’s real and what’s (iii)_______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　pretense; illusion; invented

Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)______ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)______.Scientific papers often (i)______ what actually happened in the course of the investigations they describe. Misunderstandings, blind alleys, and mistakes of various sorts will fail to appear in the final written accounts, because (ii)______ is a desirable attribute when transmitting results in a science report and would be poorly served by (iii)______.

答案：A, F　pugnacious; truculent

Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.Now that photographic prints have become a popular field for collecting, auctions are becoming more (i)______. It is not just the entry of new collectors into the field that is causing this intensification. Established collectors’ interests are also becoming more (ii)______. Those who once concentrated on the work of either the nineteenth-century pioneers or the twentieth-century modernists are now keen to have (iii)______ collections.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, G　competitive; wide-ranging; comprehensive

Because modern-day crinoids usually lose and regenerate their arms as a result of attacks by other marine animals, scientists have reasoned that evidence of arm regeneration in fossil crinoids might be __________ of past predator-prey interactions.Because modern-day crinoids usually lose and regenerate their arms as a result of attacks by other marine animals, scientists have reasoned that evidence of arm regeneration in fossil crinoids might be __________ of past predator-prey interactions.
答案：E, F　an indicator; a hint

#154 TC DOUBLE
Orchestra players are not always the best judges of conductors. Not surprisingly, when evaluating conductors they put a high premium on musicianship and (i) __________ and therefore typically gravitate toward conductors who are both technically competent and (ii) __________. But the ability to galvanize a hundred musicians into giving a great performance requires more than proficiency and affability.Orchestra players are not always the best judges of conductors. Not surprisingly, when evaluating conductors they put a high premium on musicianship and (i) __________ and therefore typically gravitate toward conductors who are both technically competent and (ii) __________. But the ability to galvanize a hundred musicians into giving a great performance requires more than proficiency and affability.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　collegiality; personally likable
impartial
impartial: standard unit headword
#155 TC TRIPLE
The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.The author's new novel is everything that her first was not: obvious and (i) __________ rather than understated and subtly shaded. Instead of quietly revealing themselves through tiny, unexpected gestures, the characters are forced to make (ii) __________ statements that announce exactly what they are thinking and serve primarily to underscore the author's sociological points. There are occasional glimpses of the (iii) __________ and emotional wisdom so lavishly demonstrated in the author's previous novel--gifts, the reader hopes, that will be more fruitfully employed in her next.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, F, I　melodramatic; tendentious; psychological acuity
intuitive
intuitive: standard unit headword
#156 Text Completion
For many years Cole experienced (i)______ his professional circle. He was a (ii)______ figure in the Middle East Studies Association of North America, editing for five years its flagship publication, and in 2004 he was even elected the association’s incoming president. But because his research focused on highly (iii)______ aspects of the eighteenth-and-nineteenth century Middle East, he was unlikely to achieve any sort of public acclaim.For many years Cole experienced (i)______ his professional circle. He was a (ii)______ figure in the Middle East Studies Association of North America, editing for five years its flagship publication, and in 2004 he was even elected the association’s incoming president. But because his research focused on highly (iii)______ aspects of the eighteenth-and-nineteenth century Middle East, he was unlikely to achieve any sort of public acclaim.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　anonymity outside; leading; esoteric
esoteric
esoteric: standard unit headword
#157 Sentence Equivalence
Flawed as it may be for it is constructed by subjective scientists, science itself has methods that help us ______ our bias and talk about objective reality with some validity.Flawed as it may be for it is constructed by subjective scientists, science itself has methods that help us ______ our bias and talk about objective reality with some validity.
答案：A, F　bypass; circumvent
bias
bias: standard unit headword
#158 SE
While most Centaurs (minor planets) are thought to be __________, astronomers have observed patterns of brightening from the Centaur Chiron, as well as activity similar to that of a streaking comet.While most Centaurs (minor planets) are thought to be __________, astronomers have observed patterns of brightening from the Centaur Chiron, as well as activity similar to that of a streaking comet.
答案：B, F　quiescent; dormant
homogeneous
homogeneous: standard unit headword

The (i)______ current-generation solar cells are (ii)______: although experimental cells have reached efficiencies greater than 40 percent, most commercially available cells in the early part of the twenty-first century still struggle to get past about 20 percent.The (i)______ current-generation solar cells are (ii)______: although experimental cells have reached efficiencies greater than 40 percent, most commercially available cells in the early part of the twenty-first century still struggle to get past about 20 percent.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　limitations of; clear
limitation
limitation: standard unit headword

The weather was long seen as (i) ____________ topic of conversation. But that has changed in recent years, as charged debates over such matters as global warming and acid rain have made the weather a far more (ii) ____________ matter than it used to be.The weather was long seen as (i) ____________ topic of conversation. But that has changed in recent years, as charged debates over such matters as global warming and acid rain have made the weather a far more (ii) ____________ matter than it used to be.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　an inoffensive; fraught

According to Merrin, many law school curricula are(i) the challenge of fostering an' adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with,core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a(ii) ethics, thus creating an(iii) audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.According to Merrin, many law school curricula are(i) the challenge of fostering an' adequate ethical understanding among their students. By focusing exclusively on what lawyers can or cannot get away with,core legal ethics courses often inadvertently promote a(ii) ethics, thus creating an(iii) audience for efforts to instill an appropriately broad grasp of the ethical challenges lawyers face.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　unequal to; cynical sense of; unfavorable

答案：E, F　defied; disregarded

答案：B, F　shortcoming; fault

Thus statisticians, who by virtue of their training are (ii) __________ identifying (iii) __________ interpretations of patterns in data, must increasingly bear the burden of scrutinizing data and challenging conclusions based on statistical flukes.

Although strikes remain rarer in Britain than in many other European countries, and their economic impact is (i)______ compared to the great upheavals of the 1970s and 1980s, their number has (ii)______ after a few years of somewhat greater calm.Although strikes remain rarer in Britain than in many other European countries, and their economic impact is (i)______ compared to the great upheavals of the 1970s and 1980s, their number has (ii)______ after a few years of somewhat greater calm.
Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：A, B　harmless; innocuous

#175 Text Completion
Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.Many fairy tales are complex narratives of wish fulfillment. They teach the reader that a struggle against severe difficulties in life is (i)______, that it is an intrinsic part of human existence, and that if one does not (ii)______, but steadfastly meets unexpected and often unjust hardships, one masters all obstacles and at the end (iii)______.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　unavoidable; shy away; emerges victorious

British composer Benjamin Britter would seem to have ______ place in the cultural pantheon, yet, at the same time that place is still contested by some.British composer Benjamin Britter would seem to have ______ place in the cultural pantheon, yet, at the same time that place is still contested by some.
答案：B, E　an assured; a settled

答案：B　a neutrality

Consequently he never accepted an academic position or wrote a comprehensive textbook, so that despite his years of intellectual leadership he never established a school of followers or articulated a distinctly Gilbertian creed.

Blank 2

答案：B, E　persistence; survival

Doubts and worries beset technical and scientific specialists, as well as the public at large. I do not consider such worries (ii)______, though they are often based on intuitive feeling rather than on strictly logical arguments.

The idea shows that more (iii)______ methods might compete with budding quantum cryptography.With the grand ambition of sending unbreakable coded messages, some physicists are using exotic tools—quantum mechanics and streams of individual photos—to shut out (i)______. Blank 2
Blank 3

However, they simultaneously have (i)______ certain tasks basic to the human experience, including spatial orientation and object recognition, and in so doing, have shown us how (ii)______ such fundamental skills truly are.

Blank 2

Given such a (i)______ historical fact, it will certainly be seen as (ii)______ that he should now embark on publishing a magazine ostensibly dedicated to history.Throughout his career he has never displayed a disinterested respect for the truth—for what was actually said and done rather than for what the wishes bad been. Given such a (i)______ historical fact, it will certainly be seen as (ii)______ that he should now embark on publishing a magazine ostensibly dedicated to history.
Blank 1
Blank 2

This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)______ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.Making the shift to the 90-nanometer manufacturing process has been (i)______ for semiconductor companies. This process effectively doubles the manufacturing capacity of the industry, but it (ii)______ enormous technical challenges because some components of the new semiconductor chips are no more than five to seven molecules thick.

Blank 2

答案：B, F　perspicacity; discernment

答案：B, D　constraining; deterring

Blank 1
Blank 2

#206 Text Completion
As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relatively have been astonishingly (i)______ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii)______ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii)______ , a fact that has disallowed theorists—at least so far—from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.As theories, quantum field theory and theory of general relatively have been astonishingly (i)______ that no experiment yet has shown a crack in either of them, even though both of them (ii)______ wildly counterintuitive properties in physical reality. And yet even proponents note that the two theories are (iii)______ , a fact that has disallowed theorists—at least so far—from incorporating both into a self-consistent theory of quantum gravity.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　successful; posit; deeply at odds with each

Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not ______ his moderate political record.Even though his opponent is currently trying to portray him as a wild-eyed radical, voters will likely reject this charge because it does not ______ his moderate political record.
答案：B, F　conform to; square with

Studies of conservation attitudes of people in South Africa and Tanzania have found a positive correlation between affluence and conservation attitudes, but it is unlikely that an improvement in the living standards of communities near protected areas will inevitably (iii)______ the long-term viability of many species within the protected areas.A (i)______ assumption of the model is that (ii)______ the living standards of people living adjacent to a protected area will necessarily enhance conservation within the protected area. Studies of conservation attitudes of people in South Africa and Tanzania have found a positive correlation between affluence and conservation attitudes, but it is unlikely that an improvement in the living standards of communities near protected areas will inevitably (iii)______ the long-term viability of many species within the protected areas.

Blank 2
Blank 3

答案：B, C　disparate; heterogeneous

#211 Text Completion
To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.To the avid reader of E. O Wilson, much of his most recent book Consilience: The Unity of Knowledge will be (i)______, as the book represents the culmination of a life spent thinking about everything from the social lives of ants to the social lives of people. Nonetheless, new thoughts have been mixed in with the old to produce a book remarkable for its (ii)______ and ambition.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, D　predictable; purview
remarkable
remarkable: standard unit headword
#212 Text Completion
Trying to fix problems that affect vast numbers of people has an intuitive appeal that politicians and policymakers find (i)______ , but several warehouses of research studies show that intuition is often a poor guide to fixing (ii)______ problems.Trying to fix problems that affect vast numbers of people has an intuitive appeal that politicians and policymakers find (i)______ , but several warehouses of research studies show that intuition is often a poor guide to fixing (ii)______ problems.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　irresistible; systemic
intuitivesystemicirresistible
intuitive: standard unit headword | systemic: standard unit headword | irresistible: standard unit headword

The author engages this issue from diverse perspectives, supports his arguments with many examples, and manages to avoid antagonizing others in dealing with a very ______ subject.The author engages this issue from diverse perspectives, supports his arguments with many examples, and manages to avoid antagonizing others in dealing with a very ______ subject.
答案：A, C　contentious; controversial
intrigue
intrigue: standard unit headword
#214 Sentence Equivalence
There is frequently a protracted time interval between the introduction of an innovative musical composition and its public acceptance: the concert-going public often spurns the ______ in favor of the familiar for a prolonged period.There is frequently a protracted time interval between the introduction of an innovative musical composition and its public acceptance: the concert-going public often spurns the ______ in favor of the familiar for a prolonged period.
答案：C, D　novel; original
comprehensive
comprehensive: standard unit headword
#215 Text Completion
All of these writers rely on (i)______ and (ii)______ to make their argument: they take a single idea and illustrate it with endless, rather overblown examples while avoiding any genuinely nuanced analysis.All of these writers rely on (i)______ and (ii)______ to make their argument: they take a single idea and illustrate it with endless, rather overblown examples while avoiding any genuinely nuanced analysis.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　simplification; exaggeration
overblown
overblown: standard unit headword
#216 Text Completion
The great (i)______ of most books that examine the American presidency is their ideological bias, but for the most part, this volume on the presidency maintains an impressive degree of (ii)______.The great (i)______ of most books that examine the American presidency is their ideological bias, but for the most part, this volume on the presidency maintains an impressive degree of (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　limitation; objectivity
biaslimitation
bias: standard unit headword | limitation: standard unit headword
#217 Sentence Equivalence
One of the peculiarities of humans is that we irrationally gravitate to the predictable and avoid risk; whatever the reasons for this ______, it is hardly a sound basis for dealing with complex, long-term problems.One of the peculiarities of humans is that we irrationally gravitate to the predictable and avoid risk; whatever the reasons for this ______, it is hardly a sound basis for dealing with complex, long-term problems.
答案：B, D　predilection; proclivity
vacillation
vacillation: standard unit headword
#218 Sentence Equivalence

答案：C, E　mixed; inconsistent
inexplicable
inexplicable: standard unit headword

The great (i)______ of most books that examine the American presidency is their ideological bias, but for most part, this volume on the presidency maintains an impressive degree of (ii)______ .The great (i)______ of most books that examine the American presidency is their ideological bias, but for most part, this volume on the presidency maintains an impressive degree of (ii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：B, F　indifference; apathy
timidity
timidity: standard unit headword
#221 Text Completion
Our contemporary preference for natural expression over artifice is (i)______ the traditional Western view that the skill with which you expressed a position corresponded closely to the credibility of your argument. Rhetorical styles might vary, but style itself was never a matter of (ii)______. And “style” was not just a well-turned sentence: poor expression, it was believed, (iii)______ poor thought.Our contemporary preference for natural expression over artifice is (i)______ the traditional Western view that the skill with which you expressed a position corresponded closely to the credibility of your argument. Rhetorical styles might vary, but style itself was never a matter of (ii)______. And “style” was not just a well-turned sentence: poor expression, it was believed, (iii)______ poor thought.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, E, I　a departure from; indifference; betrayed

contemporary: standard unit headword
#222 Sentence Equivalence
The ______ nature of the approval process for new drugs suggests that incentives to promote more expeditious decision making may be necessary.The ______ nature of the approval process for new drugs suggests that incentives to promote more expeditious decision making may be necessary.
答案：D, F　sluggish; dilatory
arbitrary
arbitrary: standard unit headword
#223 Text Completion
To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory data
bear
bear: standard unit headword

Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to ______ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.Compared to their predecessors, who were more nationalist than feminist in political orientation, Roman women activists of the younger generation are more inclined to ______ women’s rights, often creating organizations focused primarily on women’s issues.
答案：C, F　champion; defend
de-emphasize
de-emphasize: standard unit headword

To get funding and tenured positions, medical researchers have to get their work published in well-regarded journals, where rejection can climb above 90 percent. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. Not surprisingly, the studies that tend to make the grade are those that make (i)______ claims. But while coming up with such (ii)______ claims is relatively easy, getting the data to bear them out is another matter. When studied rigorously, the great majority of these claims (iii)______ .

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, G　eye-catching; striking; yield contradictory evidence

答案：A, D　glut; surfeit

revival: standard unit headword
#227 Text Completion
Catherine the Great comes across in her memoirs as (i)______ ruler with a razor-sharp intellect, letting nothing stand in the way of her ambitions. In short, the impression the memoirs give is entirely in accord with her reputation for being (ii)______.Catherine the Great comes across in her memoirs as (i)______ ruler with a razor-sharp intellect, letting nothing stand in the way of her ambitions. In short, the impression the memoirs give is entirely in accord with her reputation for being (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　an uncompromising; formidable
accord
accord: standard unit headword
#228 Sentence Equivalence
Although one can adduce myriad of examples of ecosystem disruption by nonindigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survived in fact appear to have quite ______ effects on the ecosystem they have invaded.Although one can adduce myriad of examples of ecosystem disruption by nonindigenous species, nevertheless most introduced species that survived in fact appear to have quite ______ effects on the ecosystem they have invaded.
答案：C, E　minimal; trifling
intrigue
intrigue: standard unit headword
#229 Text Completion
Human brains are amazing organs but they are not perfect. Cognitive biases, as we know, sometimes produce errors. Yet the term "error" could be (i)______ in that it suggests that there might be (ii)______ way in which cognition should work. Such a binary opposition is (iii)______ .Human brains are amazing organs but they are not perfect. Cognitive biases, as we know, sometimes produce errors. Yet the term "error" could be (i)______ in that it suggests that there might be (ii)______ way in which cognition should work. Such a binary opposition is (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, H　problematic; a uniquely correct; reductive
bias
bias: standard unit headword
#230 Sentence Equivalence
The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through ______ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.The sociologist argued that criminal behavior is an impermanent condition because it is the result of cyclical forces operating through ______ factors, not the manifestation of deeply rooted personal characteristics.
答案：A, C　contingent; circumstantial
alarming
alarming: standard unit headword
#231 Text Completion
Investors are grateful that the attorney general has stepped in to pursue inquiries into the misfeasance in the financial markets, given that the regulators officially charged with policing the industry have been ______.Investors are grateful that the attorney general has stepped in to pursue inquiries into the misfeasance in the financial markets, given that the regulators officially charged with policing the industry have been ______.
答案：B　diffident
charge
charge: standard unit headword
#232 Sentence Equivalence
It is commonly said that each person’s aesthetic taste is ______, yet that notion overlooks a large body of shared aesthetic preferences.It is commonly said that each person’s aesthetic taste is ______, yet that notion overlooks a large body of shared aesthetic preferences.
答案：C, E　distinct; idiosyncratic
inexplicable
inexplicable: standard unit headword

Most capuchin monkey conflict involves such a (i)______ repertoire of gestural and vocal signals that it is difficult for researchers to tease apart the meanings of the individual signals. This (ii)______ is (iii)______ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.Most capuchin monkey conflict involves such a (i)______ repertoire of gestural and vocal signals that it is difficult for researchers to tease apart the meanings of the individual signals. This (ii)______ is (iii)______ by the fact that many signals seem to shift in meaning according to the context in which they are produced and the developmental stage of the individuals producing them.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, G　rich; problem; augmented

accord: standard unit headword

答案：A, B　utilitarian; functional
ornamental
ornamental: standard unit headword

答案：C, E　private; insular

settled: standard unit headword

History teaches us that science is not ______ enterprise; indeed, it is quite the opposite, a motley assortment of tools designed to safeguard researchers against their own biases.History teaches us that science is not ______ enterprise; indeed, it is quite the opposite, a motley assortment of tools designed to safeguard researchers against their own biases.
答案：E　a monolithic
bias

The recent exhibition on Dadaism is nothing if not ______, for the visual arts are currently awash in Dadaist gestures and gambits of one variety or another.The recent exhibition on Dadaism is nothing if not ______, for the visual arts are currently awash in Dadaist gestures and gambits of one variety or another.
答案：B, E　timely; opportune

misguided: standard unit headword
#239 Text Completion
Industry-sponsored scientific research on chemical safety often (i)______. Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)______ that research. Even though the source of funding has been determined to be a less significant cause of bias than other factors, industry support suffices, in the minds of many people, to (iii)______ the credibility of scientific work.Industry-sponsored scientific research on chemical safety often (i)______. Media reports regularly imply that industry support of scientific work is alone sufficient to (ii)______ that research. Even though the source of funding has been determined to be a less significant cause of bias than other factors, industry support suffices, in the minds of many people, to (iii)______ the credibility of scientific work.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　elicits skepticism; invalidate; vitiate

答案：A, B　minimal; trifling

#241 Text Completion
The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.The perennial problem for critics of nineteenth-century novelist Charlotte Yonge is the relationship of her realism to her (i)______. While admiring her complex psychological portraits and detailed descriptions of quotidian family life, readers since her own time have tended to fault her improbable manipulation of plot to teach a moral lesson. Indeed, many critics (ii)______ her because of her willingness to (iii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, D, H　didacticism; dismiss; sacrifice credibility
fault
fault: standard unit headword
#242 Sentence Equivalence
There are many ways in which rat brains and human brains are ______; indeed, rat brains are often used as generalized models for all mammalian brains, including our own.There are many ways in which rat brains and human brains are ______; indeed, rat brains are often used as generalized models for all mammalian brains, including our own.
答案：B, D　analogous; comparable
inexplicable
inexplicable: standard unit headword
#243 Text Completion
If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve ______ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.If the study proves that bears are still endemic to the area, the proposal to introduce additional bears of the same species will probably face less opposition since the plan would then involve ______ a historic population, not trying to build a population from scratch.
答案：B　augmenting
bear
bear: standard unit headword
#244 Sentence Equivalence
There are great ______ in countries’ greenhouse gas emissions, especially in per capita terms; while the United States and China are similar in aggregate emissions, United States’ per capita emissions are a huge multiple of China’s.There are great ______ in countries’ greenhouse gas emissions, especially in per capita terms; while the United States and China are similar in aggregate emissions, United States’ per capita emissions are a huge multiple of China’s.
答案：B, E　disparities; variances

The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______ he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.The notion of film producers as the ogres of the movie business has proved an (i)______ one, but according to The Producers by Tim Adler, it is not always grounded in reality. Attacking what he calls the “auteur myth”—the idea of the director as the only purveyor of art in an industry otherwise peopled with (ii)______ he places at the heart of his book an image of the producer, not the director, as the primary (iii)______ force in the development and production of a movie.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, E, I　enduring; profit mongers; creative

We do not always use words in accordance with their dictionary definitions, for meaning often fluctuates with context; that does not mean, however, that we are completely ______ in how we use language.We do not always use words in accordance with their dictionary definitions, for meaning often fluctuates with context; that does not mean, however, that we are completely ______ in how we use language.
答案：B, E　unconstrained; free

irrational: standard unit headword

答案：D　unqualified

Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.Communal feeding is a remarkable behavioral aspect of this generally solitary animal. It is also misunderstood behavior and one of the reasons that Tasmanian devils have a bad reputation. Far from being a (i)______, communal devil feeding is (ii)______ and purposeful, and is described as (iii)______ behavior. The screaming and apparent fighting is an elaborate combination and variety of vocalizations and postures by which order is maintained.

Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, I　free-for-all; structured; ritualized

#261 Text Completion
The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)______ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)______ , for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)______ .The art of letter writing is like the art of acting in that it is the impression of (i)______ which usually makes a performance convincing. Great letter writers, like great actors, have a gift for (ii)______ , for the here and now, the depth of expression depending on its closeness to actual processes of thought. By these criteria Ellen Terry was a remarkable letter writer indeed: her letters invariably have an air of (iii)______ .
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, F, H　spontaneity; immediacy; improvisation
remarkable
remarkable: standard unit headword
#262 Text Completion
Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?Until now, old snapshots and home movies faded and crumbled and were eventually (i)______. Only a few precious mementos were preserved and passed along. But as photography moves into the digital realm, family albums and home videos seem capable of (ii)______; our capacity to store them is, for all practical purposes, approaching the infinite. Is such a transformation a good thing? The natural world teaches us that (iii)______ are vital to ecological health. Does a similar principle apply to communal memory?
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, G　discarded; immortality; death and decay

However, Kenny would have added significantly to his book’s value had he more effectively (ii)______ the influence of ancient philosophy on the subsequent tradition. As it is, newcomers to the subject will have little (iii)______ the afterlife enjoyed by ancient philosophy in the period 1600-1750.In this single volume, Kenny aims to survey for the general reader all of ancient philosophy. Understandably, space in such a book is (i)______ and he is not to be faulted for minor omissions. #306 Text Completion
An affinity for (i)______ is a characteristic of Bynum's work and of her rhetorical strategy as a historian, and it is often both refreshing and illuminating. But this predilection for (ii)______ can be vexing, too, by appearing to eschew necessary distinctions.An affinity for (i)______ is a characteristic of Bynum's work and of her rhetorical strategy as a historian, and it is often both refreshing and illuminating. But this predilection for (ii)______ can be vexing, too, by appearing to eschew necessary distinctions.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, F　paradox; unresolvable ambivalences
indiscriminate
indiscriminate: standard unit headword
#307 Text Completion
The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)______ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) ______ as the articles themselves.The introductions to each section, written by the editors of the anthology, provide useful background material, but they do not provide critical analysis of the articles. Because the articles are in many senses the editors’ personal favorites, it is probably (i)______ to expect more from the introductions—but if the book is to be read by advanced students, such criticism would be as (ii) ______ as the articles themselves.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, F　unrealistic; informative
bias
bias: standard unit headword
#308 Text Completion
Although Sapir and Whorf’s notion that different languages may impart different cognitive skills met with (i)______ early on, they had little evidence to support their claim. By the 1970s many scientists had become (ii)______ their hypothesis, and it was largely (iii)______ as a new set of theories claiming that language and thought are universal came to dominate the field.Although Sapir and Whorf’s notion that different languages may impart different cognitive skills met with (i)______ early on, they had little evidence to support their claim. By the 1970s many scientists had become (ii)______ their hypothesis, and it was largely (iii)______ as a new set of theories claiming that language and thought are universal came to dominate the field.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, E, I　excitement; disenchanted with; abandoned
revive
revive: standard unit headword
#309 Text Completion
Unlike the elected branches of the United States government, where making personal connection with citizens is (i)______ and almost (ii)______ political efficacy, the United States Supreme Court continues to maintain that its members should communicate with the public almost exclusively through formal opinions—and even then through ceremonial rituals that date back to the nineteenth century.Unlike the elected branches of the United States government, where making personal connection with citizens is (i)______ and almost (ii)______ political efficacy, the United States Supreme Court continues to maintain that its members should communicate with the public almost exclusively through formal opinions—and even then through ceremonial rituals that date back to the nineteenth century.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　rampant; a requirement for

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：C, D　elitist; undesirable

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　relish; insufferable

#312 Text Completion
Slight but ______ variations in the timing of the star’s light pulses led astronomers to deduce that it was being pulled backward and forward by three planets orbiting around it.Slight but ______ variations in the timing of the star’s light pulses led astronomers to deduce that it was being pulled backward and forward by three planets orbiting around it.
答案：B　regular

(i)______ considerations, however, make it difficult to (ii)______ oxytocin levels during sports events: few will willingly interrupt player in the middle of a game in order for scientists to test players’ blood.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：A, E　practical; quantify changes in

It was fine to be (i)______ in the old days when papers were still not losing readers and ad revenue was not tough to come by, but many editors today are uneasy about bludgeoning their readers with the inherently (ii)______ work of cartoonists.It was fine to be (i)______ in the old days when papers were still not losing readers and ad revenue was not tough to come by, but many editors today are uneasy about bludgeoning their readers with the inherently (ii)______ work of cartoonists.
Blank 1
Blank 2

答案：E　a simplification of

#316 Text Completion
Many legislators who helped Roosevelt shape the New Deal ______ the fact that emerging social problems affected every segment of the population; nonetheless, they often acted with a view to aiding only their own constituents.Many legislators who helped Roosevelt shape the New Deal ______ the fact that emerging social problems affected every segment of the population; nonetheless, they often acted with a view to aiding only their own constituents.
答案：B　bemoaned

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, F　sound; intrinsically

Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, E　intensity; iconoclastic

The author of this political history text shows considerable bias against the political party when assigning credit or blame for its actions: he deems (i)______ what he favors and avoidable what he (ii)______.The author of this political history text shows considerable bias against the political party when assigning credit or blame for its actions: he deems (i)______ what he favors and avoidable what he (ii)______.
Blank 1
Blank 2
答案：B, D　inevitable; condemns

#320 Text Completion
Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.Recent proposals for fixing the climate have taken the form of large-scale geoengineering projects such as launching mirrors into space to reflect solar radiation away from Earth, undertakings that are vastly more (i)______ than anything a nineteenth-century rainmaker could have cooked up. What is unclear, as one looks back at the history of weather modification research, is whether this resourceful ambition will be (ii)______, or if, by contrast, it serves to make the scientific community’s (iii)______ that much more devastating.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：B, F, H　enterprising; productive; impotence
accessiblemirror
accessible: standard unit headword | mirror: standard unit headword

Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)______ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)______ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)______. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.Cultures can shape attitudes and beliefs in ways that (i)______ conscious awareness or control; in other words, cultural orientations may develop from processes that do not entail (ii)______ participation, and cultures may pervade subtle psychological dynamics in ways that individuals may not be able to (iii)______. Thus, theories and tools developed to study implicit cognition may increase our understanding of the complex interplay between culture and individuals.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：A, D, G　operate outside of; active; report

#325 Text Completion
The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been to a large extent, (i)______ . These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)______ is not (iii)______ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.The motives of many major investors in Pop Art have arguably been to a large extent, (i)______ . These collectors demonstrate and enhance their power over the art market by establishing seemingly arbitrary works of art as priceless. This phenomenon reveals that (ii)______ is not (iii)______ of truth or beauty, but simply a trick of investment capital.
Blank 1
Blank 2
Blank 3
答案：C, D, G　self-aggrandizing; value; a product
